title: StitchFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing Stitch features in a component and supports the ability to create new Stitch features.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a StitchFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the stitch feature you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the StitchFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified stitch feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified stitch feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of Stitch features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Stitch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new stitch feature.

add.title: StitchFeatures.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created StitchFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A StitchFeatureInput object that defines the desired stitch feature. Use the createInput method to create a new StitchFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the StitchFeatureInput object) to define the stitch feature.

related_samples.name: Stitch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new stitch feature.

classType.title: StitchFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: StitchFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a StitchFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the stitch feature you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the StitchFeatureInput object.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = stitchFeatures_var.createInput(stitchSurfaces, tolerance)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = stitchFeatures_var.createInput(stitchSurfaces, tolerance, operation)

createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created StitchFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: stitchSurfaces
parameters.description: The surfaces (open BRepBodies) to stitch together. Stitching surfaces can form multiple closed volumes resulting in multiple solids. Stitch Surfaces can form multiple BRepShells (entirely connected set of entities) that would result in a single non-solid BRepBody.

parameters.name: tolerance
parameters.description: ValueInput object that defines the stitching tolerance. It must define a distance value.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Specifies the operation type for the result when the final result is a closed solid. Otherwise this argument is ignored.   This is an optional argument whose default value is FeatureOperations.NewBodyFeatureOperation.

related_samples.name: Stitch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new stitch feature.

item.title: StitchFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified stitch feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: StitchFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified stitch feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: StitchFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of Stitch features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = stitchFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: StitchFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = stitchFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: StitchFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = stitchFeatures_var.objectType
